name: @murasoftware/mura.js-test
description: Utility for interacting with the Mura JSON/REST API

scripts.dev: npx grunt; WEBPACK_ENV=dev npx webpack --mode production --env production
scripts.build:package: npm run dev && npm run build
scripts.publish:prod: node -e "const pkg=require('./package.json'); const latest=require('child_process').execSync('npm view '+pkg.name+' version 2>/dev/null || echo '+pkg.version, {encoding:'utf8'}).trim(); const parts=latest.split('.'); parts[2]=parseInt(parts[2])+1; pkg.version=parts.join('.'); require('fs').writeFileSync('./package.json', JSON.stringify(pkg, null, 2));" && npm run build:package && npm publish
scripts.publish:rc: node -e "const pkg=require('./package.json'); const latest=require('child_process').execSync('npm view '+pkg.name+' version --tag rc 2>/dev/null || echo '+pkg.version, {encoding:'utf8'}).trim(); const match=latest.match(/^(.+)-rc\.(\d+)$/); pkg.version = match ? match[1]+'-rc.'+(parseInt(match[2])+1) : latest+'-rc.0'; require('fs').writeFileSync('./package.json', JSON.stringify(pkg, null, 2));" && npm run build:package && npm publish --tag rc

author: Mura Software
